cli_arguments.name: --run-eval

cli_arguments.name: --seed
cli_arguments.help: Seed the live hive with the built-in fact corpus before querying.

cli_arguments.name: --demo
cli_arguments.help: Run eval locally using DistributedHiveGraph (no Azure needed).

cli_arguments.name: -o
cli_arguments.help: Path to write eval results JSON (with --run-eval or --demo).

cli_arguments.name: --connection-string
cli_arguments.help: Azure Service Bus connection string (overrides HIVE_CONNECTION_STRING).

cli_arguments.name: --ooda-eval
cli_arguments.help: Run the OODA-path eval: send questions as plain INPUT events and read answers from Azure Log Analytics (stdout → Container Apps logs). Requires --workspace-id. Uses azure-monitor-query SDK or az CLI fallback.

cli_arguments.name: --workspace-id
cli_arguments.help: Log Analytics workspace ID for --ooda-eval answer retrieval. Can also be set via LOG_ANALYTICS_WORKSPACE_ID env var.

cli_arguments.name: --container-app
cli_arguments.help: Container App name prefix to narrow Log Analytics log filter (optional, used with --ooda-eval). Can also be set via CONTAINER_APP_NAME env var.

cli_arguments.name: --answer-wait
cli_arguments.help: Seconds to wait per question for an ANSWER in Log Analytics (default: 600).

cli_arguments.name: --repeats
cli_arguments.help: Run the eval N times and report median and stddev of scores (default: 1).

cli_arguments.name: --wait-for-ready
cli_arguments.help: Wait for N AGENT_READY events before running eval. Agents publish AGENT_READY after processing FEED_COMPLETE. Use with --run-eval to trigger eval only after agents are done.

cli_arguments.name: --ready-timeout
cli_arguments.help: Max seconds to wait for AGENT_READY events (default: 14400 = 4h).

cli_arguments.name: --model
cli_arguments.help: LLM model for agents and grading

cli_arguments.name: --agents
cli_arguments.help: Number of agents for hive conditions

cli_arguments.name: --groups
cli_arguments.help: Number of groups for federated condition

cli_arguments.name: --prompt-variant
cli_arguments.help: Prompt variant number (1-5) for testing different system prompts

cli_arguments.name: --data-dir
cli_arguments.help: Directory containing layer JSON files (default: atlas_output/)

cli_arguments.name: --data-dir
cli_arguments.help: Directory containing layer JSON files (default: atlas_output/)

cli_arguments.name: --output-dir
cli_arguments.help: Directory to write rendered pages (default: docs/atlas/)

cli_arguments.name: --output
cli_arguments.help: Output directory for all JSON files

cli_arguments.name: --issues
cli_arguments.help: Number of test issues to generate

cli_arguments.name: --dry-run
cli_arguments.help: Show what would be changed without modifying files

cli_arguments.name: --check
cli_arguments.help: Check if sync is needed without modifying

cli_arguments.name: --dry-run
cli_arguments.help: Show what would be done without modifying files

cli_arguments.name: --check
cli_arguments.help: Check if hooks are in sync without modifying files

cli_arguments.name: --languages
cli_arguments.help: Comma-separated list of languages to test or 'all' (default: all)

cli_arguments.name: --keep-repos
cli_arguments.help: Keep cloned repositories after testing (for debugging)

cli_arguments.name: path
cli_arguments.help: Markdown file or directory to validate

cli_arguments.name: path
cli_arguments.help: Markdown file or directory to validate

cli_arguments.name: --repo-root
cli_arguments.help: Repository root path (auto-detected if not provided)

cli_arguments.name: --site-url
cli_arguments.help: Base URL of the site to validate (web crawling mode)

cli_arguments.name: --local
cli_arguments.help: Local markdown file or directory to validate

cli_arguments.name: --strict
cli_arguments.help: Strict validation: internal links must exist, all errors block

cli_arguments.name: --pragmatic
cli_arguments.help: Pragmatic validation: external 5xx/timeout are warnings only

cli_arguments.name: --skip-external
cli_arguments.help: Skip external link validation (local mode only)

cli_arguments.name: --max-depth
cli_arguments.help: Maximum crawl depth (web mode only, default: unlimited)

cli_arguments.name: --turns
cli_arguments.help: Number of content turns to feed

cli_arguments.name: --check-only
cli_arguments.help: Check for updates (currently the only mode)

cli_arguments.name: --auto
cli_arguments.help: Run in autonomous agentic mode with iterative loop (clarify → plan → execute → evaluate). Usage: --auto -- -p 'your task'. See docs/AUTO_MODE.md for details.

cli_arguments.name: --max-turns
cli_arguments.help: Max turns for auto mode (default: 10). Guidance: 5-10 for simple tasks, 10-15 for medium complexity, 15-30 for complex tasks.

cli_arguments.name: --append
cli_arguments.help: Append new instructions to a running auto mode session. Finds the active auto mode log directory in the current project and injects the new prompt.

cli_arguments.name: --ui
cli_arguments.help: Enable interactive UI mode for auto mode (requires Rich library). Shows real-time execution state, logs, and allows prompt injection.

cli_arguments.name: --no-reflection
cli_arguments.help: Disable post-session reflection analysis. Reflection normally runs after sessions to capture insights and learnings.

cli_arguments.name: --subprocess-safe
cli_arguments.help: Skip all staging/env updates (staging, cleanup, settings sync). Use when running as a subprocess delegate from an existing amplihack session to avoid concurrent write races on ~/.amplihack/.claude/.

cli_arguments.name: --with-proxy-config
cli_arguments.help: Path to .env file with proxy configuration (for Azure OpenAI integration with auto persistence prompt)

cli_arguments.name: --builtin-proxy
cli_arguments.help: Use built-in proxy server with OpenAI Responses API support instead of external claude-code-proxy

cli_arguments.name: --checkout-repo
cli_arguments.help: Clone a GitHub repository and use it as working directory. Supports: owner/repo, https://github.com/owner/repo, git@github.com:owner/repo

cli_arguments.name: --docker
cli_arguments.help: Run amplihack in Docker container for isolated execution

cli_arguments.name: source
cli_arguments.help: Git URL or local directory path

cli_arguments.name: --agent
cli_arguments.help: Name of the agent whose memory to export

cli_arguments.name: --storage-path
cli_arguments.help: Custom storage path for the agent's Kuzu DB

cli_arguments.name: --agent
cli_arguments.help: Name of the target agent to import into

cli_arguments.name: --storage-path
cli_arguments.help: Custom storage path for the agent's Kuzu DB

cli_arguments.name: --sdk
cli_arguments.help: SDK to use for agent execution (default: copilot)

cli_arguments.name: --multi-agent
cli_arguments.help: Enable multi-agent architecture with coordinator, memory agent, and sub-agents

cli_arguments.name: --context
cli_arguments.help: Set context variable (key=value). Values may contain special characters: -c 'task=Fix bug (#123)'

cli_arguments.name: recipe_dir
cli_arguments.help: Directory to search for recipes (default: search all known recipe directories)

cli_arguments.name: --connection-string
cli_arguments.help: Connection string for Azure Service Bus or Redis URL

cli_arguments.name: --kuzu-db
cli_arguments.help: Path to existing Kuzu database to mount

cli_arguments.name: --eval
cli_arguments.help: Comma-separated eval types: tool_use, planning, reasoning, transfer, collaborative, all

cli_arguments.name: --sdk
cli_arguments.help: Agent SDK: mini, claude, copilot, microsoft

cli_arguments.name: --model
cli_arguments.help: LLM model for the agent (default: env EVAL_MODEL or claude-opus-4-6)

cli_arguments.name: --grader-model
cli_arguments.help: LLM model for grading (default: env GRADER_MODEL or claude-opus-4-6)

cli_arguments.name: --grader-votes
cli_arguments.help: Number of grading votes per question for multi-vote stability (default: 3)

cli_arguments.name: --sdk
cli_arguments.help: SDK to use for the agent (default: mini = LearningAgent directly)

cli_arguments.name: --memory-type
cli_arguments.help: Force memory backend: auto (prefer cognitive if available), hierarchical (FlatRetrieverAdapter), cognitive (CognitiveAdapter). Default: auto.

cli_arguments.name: --skip-learning
cli_arguments.help: Skip the learning phase; use with --load-db to test questions against an existing memory DB

cli_arguments.name: --load-db
cli_arguments.help: Path to an existing memory DB directory to load instead of creating a new one. Copies the DB to the output directory before running.

cli_arguments.name: --parallel-workers
cli_arguments.help: Number of parallel workers for question answering/grading (1=sequential, max 20, default: 10)

cli_arguments.name: --flush-every
cli_arguments.help: Flush agent memory every N turns to cap cache growth. 0 disables. Agent must expose flush_memory() (default: 100).

cli_arguments.name: --restart-every
cli_arguments.help: Restart agent every N turns to free memory via del + gc.collect. 0 disables. Requires agent recreation (default: 0).

cli_arguments.name: --segment-size
cli_arguments.help: Run learning phase in subprocess segments of N turns each. Each segment runs in a NEW Python process so native memory (Kuzu C++, aiohttp) is fully freed between segments. 0 disables (default: 0).

cli_arguments.name: --turns-slice
cli_arguments.help: Internal: learn only turns START:END from a pre-generated dialogue JSON. Format: START:END (0-indexed, exclusive end). Used by --segment-size orchestrator.

cli_arguments.name: --dialogue-json
cli_arguments.help: Internal: path to pre-generated dialogue JSON file. Used with --turns-slice for subprocess segment workers.

cli_arguments.name: --skip-questions
cli_arguments.help: Skip the questioning phase (learn only). Used by segment workers.

cli_arguments.name: --answer-mode
cli_arguments.help: Answer mode: single-shot (default, proven at 97.8%%) uses optimized intent/retrieve/synthesize pipeline. agentic uses iterative PERCEIVE->REASON->ACT->LEARN loop with tool use.

cli_arguments.name: --regression-threshold
cli_arguments.help: Max regression in pp before auto-revert (default: 5.0)

cli_arguments.name: --model
cli_arguments.help: Agent model (default: env EVAL_MODEL or claude-opus-4-6)

cli_arguments.name: --grader-model
cli_arguments.help: Grader model (default: env GRADER_MODEL or claude-opus-4-6)

cli_arguments.name: --agents
cli_arguments.help: Subset of agents to run (default: all 5)

cli_arguments.name: --model
cli_arguments.help: LLM model to use (default from env EVAL_MODEL or claude-opus-4-6)

cli_arguments.name: --levels
cli_arguments.help: Specific levels to run (default: L1-L6, use --advanced for L8-L12)

cli_arguments.name: --advanced
cli_arguments.help: Include advanced levels (L8-L10: metacognition, causal, counterfactual)

cli_arguments.name: --parallel
cli_arguments.help: Run suite N times in parallel and report median scores (max 4 concurrent)

cli_arguments.name: --runs
cli_arguments.help: Alias for --parallel. Run suite N times and report median scores. Recommended: --runs 3 for stable results on high-variance levels (L2, L5, L9, L10, L11).

cli_arguments.name: --sdk
cli_arguments.help: SDK type to evaluate (default: mini). All SDKs use the same LearningAgent for learning/answering but validate SDK agent creation.

cli_arguments.name: --grader-votes
cli_arguments.help: Number of grading votes per question (default: 1). Use 3 for majority-vote grading to reduce noise on ambiguous answers.

cli_arguments.name: --loops
cli_arguments.help: Number of improvement loops per SDK (default: 5)

cli_arguments.name: --dry-run
cli_arguments.help: Run evaluation and analysis only, do not apply changes

cli_arguments.name: source
cli_arguments.help: Plugin source (Git URL or local path)

cli_arguments.name: plugin_name
cli_arguments.help: Name of the plugin to uninstall

cli_arguments.name: plugin_name
cli_arguments.help: Name of the plugin to verify

cli_arguments.name: --dry-run
cli_arguments.help: Show what would be changed without making modifications

cli_arguments.name: --entity-id
cli_arguments.help: Entity identifier (e.g., company or organization name)

cli_arguments.name: --repo-id
cli_arguments.help: Repository identifier (defaults to the repository path)

cli_arguments.name: --docs
cli_arguments.help: Generate documentation using LLM (requires API key)

cli_arguments.name: --openai-api-key
cli_arguments.help: OpenAI API key for documentation generation (overrides environment keys)

cli_arguments.name: --llm-provider
cli_arguments.help: LLM provider to use for documentation generation

cli_arguments.name: --db-type
cli_arguments.help: Database type to use (default: kuzu - embedded, no server required)

cli_arguments.name: --neo4j-uri
cli_arguments.help: Neo4j database URI (auto-spawns container if not provided)

cli_arguments.name: --kuzu-db-path
cli_arguments.help: Path to Kuzu database directory (defaults to ~/.amplihack/blarify_kuzu_db)

cli_arguments.name: --extensions-to-skip
cli_arguments.help: File extensions to skip during analysis

cli_arguments.name: --names-to-skip
cli_arguments.help: File/folder names to skip during analysis

cli_arguments.name: --only-hierarchy
cli_arguments.help: Build only the hierarchy without LSP analysis

cli_arguments.name: --max-workers
cli_arguments.help: Maximum number of worker threads for documentation generation

cli_arguments.name: --project
cli_arguments.help: Path to the project repository (defaults to auto-detect from current directory)

cli_arguments.name: --branch
cli_arguments.help: Install from this git branch via uvx before testing

recipes.name: auto-workflow
recipes.description: Autonomous multi-turn workflow - continues until task complete or max iterations

recipes.name: cascade-workflow
recipes.description: Graceful degradation workflow with 3-level fallback cascade (PRIMARY → SECONDARY → TERTIARY)

recipes.name: code-atlas
recipes.description:
Builds a comprehensive, living code atlas as multi-layer architecture documents.
Enforces the full 3-pass bug-hunt workflow. Produces 8 layers (Layers 1–8) plus
per-journey PASS/FAIL/NEEDS_ATTENTION v

recipes.name: consensus-workflow
recipes.description: Enhanced workflow with multi-agent consensus at critical decision points - 15 steps with 7 mandatory consensus gates

recipes.name: debate-workflow
recipes.description: Multi-agent structured debate for complex decisions requiring diverse perspectives

recipes.name: default-workflow
recipes.description: Complete 23-step development workflow (Steps 0-22) with full agent orchestration

recipes.name: domain-agent-eval
recipes.description: Evaluate all 5 domain agents: DomainEvalHarness + teaching evaluation + combined scores

recipes.name: guide
recipes.description: Interactive guide to amplihack features - workflows, agents, skills, and hooks

recipes.name: investigation-workflow
recipes.description: 6-phase systematic investigation workflow with parallel agent deployment for understanding code, systems, and behavior

recipes.name: long-horizon-memory-eval
recipes.description: 1000-turn memory stress test with self-improvement loop: eval -> analyze -> diagnose -> fix -> re-eval

recipes.name: n-version-workflow
recipes.description: N-version programming for critical code - generate multiple independent implementations and select best

recipes.name: oxidizer-workflow
recipes.description:
Automated Python-to-Rust migration workflow. Treats the Python codebase as
the living specification and iteratively converges toward FULL Rust parity
through a recursive, quality-seeking, goal-evaluat

recipes.name: qa-workflow
recipes.description: Minimal 3-step workflow for simple questions and informational requests

recipes.name: quality-audit-cycle
recipes.description:
Quality audit with seek/validate/fix/recurse loop.
Runs audit → multi-agent validate → fix ALL → verify fixes → re-audit.
Minimum 3 cycles. Continues if cycle 3 finds >3 medium or any high severity.
E

Interactive teaching agent for the goal-seeking agent generator and eval system.
Provides a structured 14-lesson curriculum covering agent generation, SDK selection,
multi-agent architecture, progress
